config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak individu itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menginjak kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan yang selalu menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become an excuse to ignore personal dignity and abuse power. From my perspective in China, people often understand that social stability and collective interests matter a lot, and I do value that, especially in crises where coordination is necessary. But I also think a society is not truly strong if individual rights are treated as secondary by default, since ordinary people need real protection from unfair decisions. The better approach is balance: society can set limits when there is a clear public need, but individual rights should not be sacrificed casually or permanently.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling rentan justru warga biasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama saat darurat atau untuk ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh aturan yang jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang terlalu besar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual, because in China I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify excessive control. Social stability and collective goals are important, and I do not deny that emergencies sometimes require real limits on individual freedom, but those limits must be narrow, lawful, and temporary. If individual rights can be overridden too easily, ordinary people lose protection and the collective interest becomes whatever those in power decide it is. A strong society is not one that asks people to surrender their dignity by default; it is one that can protect both order and personal rights at the same time.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling terdampak biasanya warga biasa, bukan elite. Kepentingan publik memang bisa membatasi hak dalam keadaan darurat, tetapi harus ada batas yang jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi agar tidak berubah jadi alat kekuasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu demi kolektif, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya sekaligus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society above the individual as a general rule, because in my view that easily turns into a blank check for power. From my perspective in China, people do value collective order and social stability, and I understand why that feels important, especially during crises, but I have also seen how “for the public good” can be used too broadly. Rights should be limited only when there is a clear, lawful, and temporary necessity, not as a default habit. A society is strongest when it can protect order without treating individual dignity as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
